hard.account_name: Hong Kong Aircraft Engineering Company Limited
hard.owner_title: Vice President Asia Pacific Japan & ME
hard.next_step: 50% Confidence 2nd Jul- Updated by Charlotte- RFP coming shortly- and looked at estimated costing- deal is back in motion ! ck to set up F2F and navigate execo levels Jun 6thAll of evaluation team has now departed HAECO. Carl to reach out and re-establish the new connections - from scratch- will result in delays, if indeed project is to continue. May 27- Timeline for tender and implementation phases to be confirmed in coming 2 weeks. May 11- engagement with new team expected before end of May. Impact on decision timeline. C level engagement with Carl. pr 30: Internal change as project manager leaving. Still under internal alignment. Expected RFP by May. Apr 27 still under Internal review. Registration in their vendor portal lasy week to receive RFP by beg of May. Meeting expected by beg of May. Apr 13 meeting with project leader to review next steps on Apr 15 Apr 06 additional information to be submitted (detailed processes shared with Business users) Mar 27 Demo session with EY support on Apr30. Mar 18- good meeting with some relevant clarification (possible alternative to Data Lake & direct manufacturing not in scope). POC/WS March 30, Tender by May, Decision by June. Win themes (speed, simplicity, AI execution, Industry expertise), exec engagement still to clarify. Mar 13- meeting on Tues17 to review on centralized part number requirement. Potential showstopper. POC requirements are positive for Zycus (expected session by Mar 30) Mar 02 Use cases still under review by presales Feb 09 Presales working on use cases prep - to be presented by mid-march Feb 02 NDA signed, expecting information about current classification structure (PO#) and use cases Jan 26- Uses cases by end of Jan - WS after CNY - Tender end of March. Main pain points related with Part # records and category management: Start to review with presales and PM to provide a reasonable solution. Chasing after GCPO to meet with Carl. Jan 13th Meeting next Jan 21 to align on next steps...engaging with Kpmg (for Chinease speaking use cases delivery to deliver before CNY)...Tender by end of March Dec 08th: new session on demo and implementation best practices by Dec 11. Use cases WS will follow by end of Jan. Nov 26th: Process qualified. Add' demo by Dec, WS by Feb and Tender by end of March. Positive engagement with credentials built around our ST Eng experience and I2O approach. Highly competitive as part of largest HK conglomerate Swire Holding (HK Flagship). 14th Nov: Positive engagement. Users demo expected by Dec 04. RFP by Mar26 27th Oct: New demo session requested by Nov 06. Market discovery at this stage with expected RFP by Mar. So far, good engagement based on experience with ST Eng. 13rd Oct: Demo session by Oct 21. F2F meeting in Nov Sep 30: Look for Corporate S2P Platform. Industry similar to STE. Expected Demo by Week Oct 13 followed by workshop. Expected KO by June 26
hard.ais_why:
• On Dec 11 2025, Simon Fung (HAECO procurement lead) and Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard discussed in detail Zycus' GenAI data-mapping and Agentic offerings. Captured note from Charlotte: 'Using GenAI for data mapping, normalization, deduplication, and upload of bring-your-own-format master data from 7-8 subsidiaries delivers a unified supplier golden record without altering ERP sources, eliminating manual ETL effort.' Buyer explicitly endorsing GenAI-driven data handling as the wedge.
• Per the Mar 18 2026 rep note from Guillaume Pasquet: 'Win themes (speed, simplicity, AI execution, Industry expertise), exec engagement still to clarify.' Per Sep 30 2025 meeting (Simon Fung): 'Interest in Zycus driven by ST Engineering deal, expecting aviation and MRO expertise to address strict quality assurance compliance requirements.' AI execution is named as a top-3 win theme; Simon is treating it as a differentiator vs ERP-bundled options.
• High confidence: 9 valid Avoma meetings (haeco.com domain), 2 completed with transcripts. Product list is exceptionally AI-rich (Merlin + Agentic AI + Merlin Intake + ANA + iCompliance + ML iAnalyze). Buyer Simon Fung directly probed multi-language UI + region-specific AI compliance fields. RFP expected mid-2026, and HK AI-restriction work has been called out as managed.

north_star_verdict.headline: Fragile restart: the entire evaluation team turned over by 6 Jun 2026, but Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard re-engaged on 2 Jul, took Carl's reference costing and said the RFP is coming shortly. The deal is alive again yet has no confirmed champion or economic buyer, and a 30 Aug close is not credible when the RFP has not even issued.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Sep 2025, 279 days old, still in Formal Evaluation with no stage progression in ~9 months. Close date 30 Aug 2026 is 55 days out. RFP was promised for May 2026 and never materialized; the whole evaluation team departed by 6 Jun 2026 and Carl rebuilt contact on 2 Jul. A full enterprise motion (RFP issuance, shortlist, use-case workshop, commercials, negotiation, contract) cannot complete in 55 days from a standing restart, so the date will slip 4-6 months. Trajectory is STRONGER than the prior Off Track read because the buyer replied on 2 Jul, but momentum is only just rebuilding.
evidence: Next_Step 2 Jul 2026: '50% Confidence 2nd Jul- Updated by Charlotte- RFP coming shortly- and looked at estimated costing- deal is back in motion ! ck to set up F2F and navigate execo levels.'

Task 2 Jul 2026 (email received from Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard): 'Thank you very much Carl for the speedy response and support. Understand that the numbers shared are for reference only but that is what we are looking for at this stage. We will be in touch...' - a genuine two-way buyer exchange, not just rep outreach.
Next_Step 6 Jun 2026: 'All of evaluation team has now departed HAECO. Carl to reach out and re-establish the new connections - from scratch- will result in delays, if indeed project is to continue.'
Field history 6 Jun 2026: Amount 750000 -> 450000 (by Carl Kimball) - scope/budget cut on the same day ownership moved to Carl.
Simon Fung (Group IT Business Analysis Manager, primary Evaluator) left HAECO 8 May 2026, removing the strongest technical relationship.
No Economic Buyer engaged after 9 months; no CFO or Group CPO on any activity.
Best Case forecast with no engaged EB, no confirmed champion and a fresh team reset is not defensible; honest category is Pipeline.

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated strong qualitative pain but never quantified it. Simon Fung framed the problem on 30 Sep 2025 as fragmented, non-unified procurement across '7 or 8 subsidiaries or entities' where some units are 'still using different manual ways or some office tools,' undermining the group's ability to 'leverage different entities their procurement bargaining power.' The recurring operational pain is part-number and supplier golden-record consolidation across those entities (Next_Step 26 Jan 2026: 'Main pain points related with Part # records and category management'). Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard reviewed 'estimated costing' on 2 Jul 2026, so budget scrutiny is now live, but no cost-savings target, headcount, cycle-time, error-rate or ROI figure has been stated on any call or field. Quantifying the value case for the new team is the immediate gap.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is confirmed or engaged, but the evidence points at the Group Procurement leadership. Simon Fung said on 30 Sep 2025 that 'the group procurement teams will be the business owners of the entire initiative... they will make the call as well,' placing budget authority with that function rather than IT. In the Salesforce contact roles that function is headed by Christian Pinter (Group Procurement General Manager) and Nicola Or (Head Of Procurement); Carl Kimball emailed Nicola Or directly on 1 Jul 2026 to re-establish contact. Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard (Data Analytics and Financial Performance Manager) owns the cost review per the 2 Jul exchange but is an analyst, not the budget owner. No CFO or Group CPO has appeared on any activity in nine months. Confirming who signs for the S2P mandate with the reset team is a ranked move.
sources: Task/email 1 Jul 2026 (Carl Kimball to Nicola Or)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well documented from the discovery and follow-up calls. Simon Fung stated on 30 Sep 2025 that ERP-bundled options (SAP, Oracle) were rejected because 'the aviation or the MRO context is not sufficient enough to support our operations' given 'really really really strict' quality-assurance compliance, so aviation/MRO fit and compliance depth are top criteria. On 11 Dec 2025 the buyer pressed two concrete technical criteria: (1) a supplier and part-number golden record that consolidates data across 7-8 entities without altering the source ERP data, and (2) UI/field configurability for region-specific regulatory fields ('mandatory regulation rules, say in China') plus multi-language (Mandarin) support. Charlotte added that contract management and item catalogs, not P2P/PO-PR, are the primary scope ('those transactions accurately done in ERP... procurement platform... is really source to procure'). AI execution / GenAI data mapping is an explicit differentiator criterion (AIS why, 18 Mar 2026).
sources: Avoma follow-up call 11 Dec 2025 (Simon Fung, Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer described its own process clearly on 30 Sep 2025: a generic demo to the Group Procurement team, then use-case workshops with the entity procurement users ('the group procurement teams will be the business owners... they will make the call'), then a formal tender, targeting a project kick-off around Q2 2026. That maps to the Next_Step history: demos Oct-Dec 2025, a use-case workshop planned before Chinese New Year, then a tender. The process then broke: the RFP slipped from Mar to May 2026 and never issued, and the entire evaluation team departed by 6 Jun 2026, forcing a restart. As of 2 Jul 2026 the process is being rebuilt: Charlotte says 'RFP coming shortly' and Carl is trying to 'set up F2F and navigate execo levels.' HAECO also runs a formal vendor-portal pre-tender (registration 'T-2026-0006-Group Procurement Solution', 17 Apr 2026), so a structured tender is the gating step ahead.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible because the deal is pre-RFP and has not reached commercials. What is known: HAECO buys through a formal group vendor portal and tender (pre-tender 'T-2026-0006-Group Procurement Solution', registration 17 Apr 2026), so procurement will run a structured tender before any MSA/SOW. Simon Fung flagged on 30 Sep 2025 that after direction is set 'we need to dedicate some time for the official procurement procedures... contract drafting, approval, all those stuff.' No MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec or DPA discussion has occurred. As a Hong Kong / APAC buyer a PO is likely required to gate invoicing. Getting the RFP scope and the tender timeline in writing from the new team is what would create paper-process visibility.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific, operational and confirmed by the buyer. Across 7-8 acquired subsidiaries HAECO has non-unified procurement strategy and systems, with some entities 'still using different manual ways or some office tools' (Simon Fung, 30 Sep 2025). The sharpest technical pain is the supplier and part-number golden record: the same physical supplier and the same part carry different codes across entity ERPs ('one is part a, the other part zero a, the other part a dash one'), and the buyer cannot change the source data because it is 'rooted in their ERP system' and 'very crucial to their production' (Simon Fung and Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard, 11 Dec 2025). This was flagged as a 'potential showstopper' in Next_Step 13 Mar 2026. Compliance rigor as an aviation MRO ('really strict rules') is a second, regulatory pain. The pain owner was Simon Fung (IT); with his departure the new IT/procurement owner must be re-identified.
sources: Avoma follow-up call 11 Dec 2025 (Simon Fung, Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard)

champion.narrative: There is no confirmed active champion today. Simon Fung (Group IT Business Analysis Manager, Evaluator) was the closest advocate through early 2026, driving the technical evaluation and owning the part-number showstopper, but he left HAECO on 8 May 2026 and Next_Step 6 Jun 2026 confirmed 'All of evaluation team has now departed HAECO.' Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard re-appeared on 2 Jul 2026, took the reference costing and reported the deal 'back in motion,' which is a positive coach signal, but her authority and willingness to advocate internally are unproven and she is an analytics/finance manager, not a procurement decision owner. No evidence of internal mobilization or competitive blocking exists from any current contact. Rebuilding a champion inside the reset team, most likely Simon Fung's IT successor or a Group Procurement lead, is the foundational move before the RFP lands.

competition.narrative: This is a multi-vendor evaluation inside the Swire Group conglomerate (Next_Step 26 Nov 2025: 'Highly competitive as part of largest HK conglomerate Swire Holding'), with Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua and SAP Ariba logged. The buyer gave one clear competitive signal on 30 Sep 2025: ERP-bundled suites (SAP/Oracle) were found weak because 'the aviation or the MRO context is not sufficient enough,' which disadvantages SAP Ariba. Zycus is positioned on speed, simplicity, AI execution and aviation/MRO expertise, anchored to the ST Engineering win (Guillaume Pasquet cited that ST Eng chose Zycus over iRequest, GEP and Ariba, with Coupa 'not even in the loop'). The wedge is Merlin Intake plus Agentic AI and the GenAI golden-record data mapping the buyer endorsed. The risk: the 6 Jun team turnover reset every relationship, so all four rivals return to equal footing for a fresh RFP and the new team's preferences are unknown.
sources: Avoma intro call 30 Sep 2025 (Guillaume Pasquet, Simon Fung)

deal_movement.summary: The deal stalled hard through Apr-Jun 2026 (RFP slipped from May and never issued, the entire evaluation team departed by 6 Jun, and the amount was cut from 750k to 450k) then re-engaged on 2 Jul when Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard replied to Carl, took the reference costing and reported it 'back in motion.' Momentum is rebuilding from a standing restart.

items.change: Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard re-engaged, accepted Carl's reference costing and said RFP is coming shortly - deal 'back in motion'
Carl Kimball re-established outreach to Nicola Or (Head of Procurement) to rebuild contacts after team turnover
Ownership moved from Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball; amount cut 750000 -> 450000
Entire evaluation team confirmed departed from HAECO; relationships reset

competitive_position.summary: A four-vendor field within Swire Group, but no rival is buyer-preferred on current evidence and the 6 Jun team turnover reset everyone to equal footing for a fresh RFP. GEP Worldwide is the live size/fit peer threat (a full-suite competitor also strong in APAC conglomerates). SAP Ariba is weakened by the buyer's own 30 Sep 2025 verdict that ERP-bundled suites lack aviation/MRO depth. Coupa and iValua are logged but have no recent buyer signal (Coupa was 'not even in the loop' at the ST Engineering deal). Zycus wins on aviation/MRO fit (ST Eng reference) plus Merlin Intake and Agentic AI golden-record data mapping.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Out-position GEP on native Agentic AI and Merlin Intake plus the ST Engineering aviation/MRO proof point; GEP has breadth but not the embedded AI golden-record data mapping the buyer endorsed.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: the aviation or the MRO context is not sufficient enough to support our operations
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 30 Sep 2025 (Simon Fung, on ERP-bundled suites)
competitors.how_we_win: Lean into the buyer's own rejection of ERP-bundled procurement for aviation MRO; contrast Zycus's configurable compliance fields and multi-language UI vs Ariba's ERP-tied model.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasise embedded Agentic AI and flexible configuration; Coupa was 'not even in the loop' at the comparable ST Engineering aviation win.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on AI maturity and single-platform organic build vs iValua's configuration-heavy model, tied to the part-number golden-record use case.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits the buyer's core criteria (aviation/MRO expertise, AI-driven golden-record data mapping, configurable compliance fields, contract-management-first scope) but has one open technical exposure: cross-entity part-number/item-master mapping, which Zycus said is not its core master-data strength and would need a workaround or custom development.

items.criterion: Aviation/MRO industry fit and strict quality-assurance compliance
items.quote: the aviation or the MRO context is not sufficient enough to support our operations
items.source: Avoma intro call 30 Sep 2025 (Simon Fung), positioned via ST Engineering reference

items.criterion: AI / GenAI-driven supplier golden-record data mapping across 7-8 entities
items.quote: you give your data in your own format... our Gen AI tool gives us this Zycus uploadable template filled with your data

items.criterion: Cross-entity part-number / item-master golden record without changing source ERP codes
items.quote: we typically don't really position ourselves as an item master or material master management system... it would be a custom development

items.criterion: Configurable region-specific regulatory fields and multi-language (Mandarin) support
items.quote: Fields and structures, conditional forms, request types... all that can be changed... we do support multiple languages

items.requirement: Prove cross-entity part-number golden-record mapping without altering source ERP codes
items.quote: in Zycus it will be consolidated into one single supplier records... we cannot change that part number ID because it's rooted in their ERP system
items.source: Avoma follow-up call 11 Dec 2025 (Simon Fung, Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard)

items.deliverable: Issue the RFP / tender and confirm the new evaluation team's timeline
items.grounding_quote: RFP coming shortly- and looked at estimated costing- deal is back in motion

items.quote: the group procurement teams will be the business owners... they will make the call
items.source: Avoma intro call 30 Sep 2025 (Simon Fung); no EB on any activity in 9 months

items.area: New evaluation team unmapped after full turnover
items.quote: All of evaluation team has now departed HAECO. Carl to reach out and re-establish the new connections - from scratch

items.source: Task 2 Jul 2026 (Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard); no savings/ROI figure on any call or field

best_practice_check.summary: The deal just restarted from a total team turnover; the win depends on multi-threading fast into the reset team, re-securing a technical champion, and closing the one exposed criterion before the RFP lands.
flags: Multi-thread into the reset team now: only Charlotte re-engaged and Simon Fung (technical champion) is gone, so identify his IT successor and a Group Procurement lead before the RFP issues.
Convert the ST Engineering aviation/MRO win into a written proof point for the new team to hold the aviation-fit differentiator versus GEP and Ariba.
Close the item-master / part-number golden-record exposure with a concrete workaround proposal, since Zycus flagged it as not core and the buyer called it a potential showstopper.

items.name: Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard
items.title: Data Analytics and Financial Performance Manager
items.risk: Only re-established contact; owns cost review but not budget authority; advocacy strength unproven post-turnover.

items.name: Nicola Or
items.title: Head Of Procurement
items.risk: Carl reached out 1 Jul to re-establish contact; a likely decision owner but engagement post-turnover is unconfirmed.

items.name: Christian Pinter
items.title: Group Procurement General Manager
items.risk: Highest procurement authority named and inferred budget owner for the group mandate, but never directly engaged and status after the 6 Jun turnover is unknown.

items.name: Simon Fung
items.title: Group IT Business Analysis Manager
items.risk: Was the primary Evaluator and technical relationship and owned the part-number showstopper; his departure removed the strongest advocate and must be replaced.

items.name: Joey Eley
items.title: Head of Group Procurement Governance
items.risk: Governance authority relevant to tender approval; never actively engaged and status after turnover unknown.

champion_strength.summary: No active champion. Simon Fung (Evaluator, prior technical advocate) left HAECO 8 May 2026 and the whole evaluation team departed by 6 Jun. Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard re-engaged on 2 Jul and is a positive coach, but her internal authority and advocacy are unproven. What has developed: a re-opened line of contact and live cost review; what is missing: a named internal advocate with access to the budget owner who will push Zycus through the tender.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole; Next_Step 6 Jun 2026; Task/email 2 Jul 2026 (Charlotte Lollieron-Bernard)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is landing well: the buyer directly endorsed GenAI 'bring your own format' data mapping for the golden-record use case (11 Dec 2025), and AI execution is a named top-3 win theme. Merlin Intake and Agentic AI are strong wedges versus ERP-bundled and legacy rivals, so positioning is on target, not under-played.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8.0 / Strong is corroborated by the calls: Simon Fung and Charlotte engaged deeply on GenAI data mapping, normalization and deduplication, and treated AI execution as a differentiator versus ERP-bundled options. The field is accurate, not over-rated.

items.detail: Entire evaluation team departed by 6 Jun 2026; all relationships reset and the new team's preferences are unknown.
No economic buyer or C-level sponsor engaged after 9 months; budget owner inferred (Group Procurement) but never in the room.
items.source: Avoma intro call 30 Sep 2025; no EB activity to date

items.detail: Amount cut 750000 -> 450000 on 6 Jun 2026, a defensive scope/budget narrowing that usually signals cost caution or a phased approach.
Cross-entity part-number / item-master golden-record mapping is Zycus's weakest fit area and the buyer's stated potential showstopper; may need a workaround or custom development.
RFP repeatedly promised (Mar, then May 2026) and never issued; 30 Aug close is not credible from a pre-RFP restart.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal re-warmed on 2 Jul after a cold May-Jun, but on a fragile footing: one re-engaged contact, no champion, no EB, and a pre-RFP restart. Positive re-engagement, but not yet solid momentum.
items: Buyer replied and took reference costing 2 Jul 2026 - genuine re-engagement
No champion or economic buyer confirmed post-turnover
RFP still only 'coming shortly', not issued

scope_change.detail: Amount cut 750000 -> 450000 on 6 Jun 2026; buyer de-prioritised P2P/PO-PR (Simon Fung 11 Dec 2025: 'the POPR... it's not the main focus because those transactions accurately done in ERP') and centred scope on contract management, supplier and part-number golden records; 18 Mar note also removed Data Lake and direct manufacturing from scope. A defensive narrowing consistent with budget caution or a phased implementation.

customer_preference.evidence: Deep Zycus-specific technical probing on golden-record and UI configurability (11 Dec 2025) and interest driven by the ST Engineering reference; on 2 Jul Charlotte accepted the reference costing as 'what we are looking for at this stage,' but no explicit preference over rivals and the evaluation is still multi-vendor and pre-RFP.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (fragmented procurement and non-unified part-number/supplier data across 7-8 entities, strict aviation MRO compliance) but no quantified ROI, savings, headcount or cycle-time figure; 'estimated costing' reviewed 2 Jul is a price reference, not a value case.

items.action: Lock the F2F with Charlotte and Nicola Or to confirm the new team and RFP timing.
items.trigger: Charlotte said RFP coming shortly and deal 'back in motion'; Carl to set up F2F
items.expected_effect: Re-anchors the restart to real people and dates and prevents the 2 Jul re-engagement going cold.

items.action: Identify and meet Simon Fung's IT successor to rebuild technical advocacy.
items.trigger: Simon Fung (technical evaluator) left 8 May; whole team departed 6 Jun
items.expected_effect: Restores a champion who can carry the golden-record use case through the tender.

items.action: Confirm who owns the S2P budget with the relevant stakeholder leadership (Christian Pinter / Nicola Or).
items.trigger: No economic buyer engaged after 9 months; group procurement 'will make the call'
items.expected_effect: Maps the economic buyer so the reference costing reaches the person who signs.

items.action: Send the new team the part-number golden-record workaround proposal and ST Engineering proof point.
items.trigger: Item-master mapping flagged a potential showstopper; Zycus owed the iMaster check from 11 Dec
items.expected_effect: Closes the one exposed criterion and cements the aviation/MRO differentiator before the RFP scores.

items.action: Run a competitive review positioning Merlin Intake and Agentic AI ahead of the RFP.
items.trigger: Four-vendor field (GEP, Coupa, iValua, Ariba) reset to equal footing after turnover
items.expected_effect: Frames the RFP on Zycus's AI and aviation-fit strengths before rivals re-anchor the new team.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut 750000 -> 450000 on 6 Jun 2026; buyer de-prioritised P2P/PO-PR (Simon Fung 11 Dec 2025: 'the POPR... it's not the main focus because those transactio (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 23 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 31 is carried by momentum 57 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount cut 750000 -> 450000 on 6 Jun 2026; buyer de-prioritised P2P/PO-PR (Simon Fung 11 Dec 2025: 'the POPR...….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 57 (moving forward): Next Step carries 23 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 16 is anchored on the stage win (31), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 57, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Hong Kong Aircraft Engineering Company Limited — Formal Evaluation · $450K · closes 30 Aug
cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 31.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.

bullets.text: Entire evaluation team departed by 6 Jun 2026; all relationships reset and the new team's preferences are unknown.
No economic buyer or C-level sponsor engaged after 9 months; budget owner inferred (Group Procurement) but never in the room.
Amount cut 750000 -> 450000 on 6 Jun 2026, a defensive scope/budget narrowing that usually signals cost caution or a phased approach.
Cross-entity part-number / item-master golden-record mapping is Zycus's weakest fit area and the buyer's stated potential showstopper; may need a workaround or custom…

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: A four-vendor field within Swire Group, but no rival is buyer-preferred on current evidence and the 6 Jun team turnover reset everyone to equal footing for a fresh RFP.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Lock the F2F with Charlotte and Nicola Or to confirm the new team and RFP timing. (by 13 Jul)
Identify and meet Simon Fung's IT successor to rebuild technical advocacy. (by 20 Jul)
Confirm who owns the S2P budget with the relevant stakeholder leadership (Christian Pinter / Nicola Or). (by 20 Jul)

calls_omitted: Haeco/ Zycus demo session 2025-11-06 (no transcript available)
6 further meetings on haeco.com domain not transcript-ready this run
evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities last 120d; field history to 22 Mar 2026
gaps: No economic buyer engaged or confirmed after 9 months
New evaluation team (post 6 Jun turnover) unmapped
No quantified business case / ROI stated by the buyer
Cross-entity part-number item-master mapping unresolved (Zycus flagged as non-core)
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Group Procurement')
